personalInfo.name: Satgun Singh Sodhi
personalInfo.summary: AI/ML Engineer and B.Tech CSE student at VIT Vellore (9.42 CGPA, 2027 batch) with hands-on experience in deep learning, LLM systems, computer vision, and production ML infrastructure. Built original research projects (CNN-Transformer architectures, RAG pipelines, transformer-based SLMs) and delivered unsupervised anomaly detection at scale during an ML internship at Reliance Industries Limited.

bullets.text: Engineered RMS-normalized flow deltas and cosine similarity features on sliding windows across all pairwise sensor combinations, translating physical pipeline behaviours into temporally independent, scale-invariant representations.

Built deep Autoencoder models (SELU activations, AlphaDropout, MAE loss) for unsupervised anomaly detection across 5 industrial pipeline systems, achieving up to 99.47% training accuracy and 93.05% validation accuracy on large multi-sensor time-series data.

Implemented Bayesian hyperparameter optimization from scratch using Gaussian Processes (RBF kernel) and Expected Improvement acquisition function without external libraries, auto-tuning training parameters with principled exploration-exploitation tradeoff.
Delivered dashboards, technical documentation, and presentations to mentors, leads, and stakeholders, honing cross-functional communication in a production ML environment.

projects.name: AsymPT: Asymmetric Parallel Transformer for Medical Image Segmentation

bullets.text: Designed a hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture with asymmetric component placement: CNNs at high resolutions, a Parallel Hybrid Block with learnable Gated Fusion at mid-scale, and Dual-Swin Transformer blocks at the bottleneck for global context modelling.

Outperformed TransUNet (77.48% Dice, 106B FLOPs) with 84% Dice Score at 28B FLOPs on the Synapse Multi-Organ Segmentation benchmark (9 classes), achieving a 6.5pp accuracy improvement with 73.6% compute reduction.
Implemented production-ready training pipeline with mixed-precision AMP, deep supervision (3-level weighted loss), cosine annealing LR scheduler, and multi-format inference support (NIfTI, PNG, NPY).

projects.name: SLM: Financial Sentiment Analysis via Transformer Architecture

bullets.text: Built a transformer-based Small Language Model (SLM) from scratch in PyTorch using GPT-2 tokenizer (Tiktoken) for financial sentiment analysis, classifying text as positive/negative/neutral and mapping outputs to actionable buy/sell/hold trading signals.

Implemented Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) for parameter-efficient fine-tuning on FinancialPhraseBank and FiQA datasets, reducing trainable parameters from millions to thousands while preserving model expressiveness on domain-specific financial language.
Applied instruction tuning to align model outputs with standardized sentiment categories, enabling robust inference on financial jargon, negations, and implicit market cues.

projects.name: DasAI: AI-Powered Discord Assistant with RAG

bullets.text: Implemented a full Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline with Sentence Transformer embeddings (384-dim) stored in Supabase pgvector for semantic search over conversation history.

Integrated Hugging Face Inference API (Llama-3.2-3B) with DuckDuckGo web search augmentation for real-time grounding, and implemented rolling conversation summaries (every 5 messages) to manage long-term context within LLM token window constraints.
Deployed with multi-tenant architecture featuring per-server data isolation and role-based access control using Supabase service roles; containerized on Railway with a Vercel dashboard.

Built a 5-stage multimodal machine vision pipeline converting raw screenshots into structured JSON/Markdown for downstream LLM consumption, achieving ~287ms end-to-end latency: preprocessing → RT-DETR object detection → EasyOCR text extraction → layout analysis → context assembly.
Selected RT-DETR over YOLOv8 for its transformer detection head capturing global layout relationships; implemented hybrid detector-fallback strategy for low-confidence edge cases.
Shipped as a pip-installable Python library with a full pytest suite, CLI interface, fine-tuning guide, and API documentation — designed for team-level integration and reproducibility.
Served as Point of Contact for CodeHub hackathon and anchored 5+ technical events including a keynote with Love Babbar (500+ attendees).
Achieved 1st Place at Binary Battles competition at Gravitas, VIT's annual technical fest.
